description: Enact the ceremony-entry identity establishment decision: a substrate-lifecycle ceremony that can reach an identity-stamping write (update, install, resolve-blocked) establishes substrate identity at entry when config.json lacks a valid substrate_id — mint via mintSubstrateId, persist through the sanctioned config write path, register via registerSubstrate — at the same entry seam as the config-migration seeding, before the first write that stamps identity; each ceremony's result reports the establishment (substrateIdEstablished carrying the minted id). Pre-identity substrates thereby heal on the sanctioned ceremony instead of refusing: the two skipped S4 migrate-path tests re-green (version bump with a shipped chain on a pre-identity substrate now migrates, and check-status reports it in-sync after), and dry/live parity is restored for the pre-identity class (both runs agree: migrated). The refusal-classification test cells that used the pre-identity substrate as their non-validation-throw trigger are re-anchored to a ceremony-independent write-boundary throw; the classification contract is unchanged. writeBlockForDir's stamping guard stays load-bearing for any write reaching it without identity (defense in depth; no lazy mint inside block writes).
acceptance_criteria: update, install, and resolve-blocked on a substrate whose config.json lacks a valid substrate_id establish identity at entry (minted, persisted via the sanctioned config write, registered in the project registry) and report it in the ceremony result; a substrate with an established identity is untouched (no re-mint, result field absent).

The two previously-skipped S4 migrate-path tests are re-greened as written or replaced per the contract: a pre-identity version bump with a shipped chain migrates (items intact, envelope advanced) and a follow-up check-status reports the schema in-sync; dry and live runs of the former pre-identity reproduction agree (both migrated).

The refusal-classification cells still pass against a ceremony-independent non-validation write-boundary throw (write-failed reason, no markers, no pending record; resolve-blocked all-or-nothing restore).

The update, install, and resolve-blocked op description/promptSnippet strings, both READMEs, and the skill narrative state the establishment behavior; CHANGELOG carries the entry; build, check, and the full test suite pass at zero failures with the change in dist/.
notes: Closes the pre-identity resync gap per the enacted ceremony-entry identity decision; the milestone's last open member path.
